titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Brooklyn city and telephone directories BCMS.0064
titlestmt.author: Brooklyn Collection staff and Dee Bowers

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-05-21 17:12:02 +0000.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

did.abstract: Brooklyn and New York City directories, including telephone books, white pages, yellow pages, and address directories ("criss-cross"). These directories are available digitally, on microfilm, and in bound volumes at both the Central library and the Center for Brooklyn History.
did.physloc: Please note that these materials are located both at the Central library and at the Center for Brooklyn History.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: These materials are located both at the Central library and at the Center for Brooklyn History. Researchers should utilize the library's Book A Librarian process to request access to the collection.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged in series by type and then format. The directories are held in three formats: digital, microfilm, and bound volumes. Within each format, the arrangement is chronological.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: The collection's digital surrogates should be used in place of the originals whenever possible.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Identification of item, date, page number; Brooklyn city and telephone directories, BCMS.0064; Brooklyn Public Library, Center for Brooklyn History.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This finding aid was created and edited by Brooklyn Collection staff over several years. The current version was entered into ArchivesSpace by Dee Bowers in May 2026.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials

p.extref: Digitized directories from this collection on the Internet Archive

relatedmaterial.p: Kings County Historic Records Microfilm Collection (BCMS.0047)
CBH holds additional directories that were part of the collections at the former Brooklyn Historical Society. Please consult CBH staff for more information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Brooklyn city and telephone directories provide a wealth of information about individuals, businesses, occupations, advertising, and social class status, as well as practical neighborhood information such as street maps, public services, shopping and restaurant guides, maps of rapid transit systems, and more. The earliest city directories list names, addresses, and occupations of Brooklyn residents, while later directories include telephone numbers for individuals and businesses. The "elite" directories list names and addresses of Brooklyn's wealthiest residents, and some include separate listings for wealthy residents of the town of Flatbush and Long Island.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: While many items at the Center for Brooklyn History are unrestricted, we do not own reproduction rights to all materials. Be aware of the several kinds of rights that might apply: copyright, licensing and trademarks. The researcher assumes all responsibility for copyright questions.

did.unittitle: Brooklyn and New York City Directories

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Brooklyn city directories had various publishers and list names, addresses, and occupations of residents of Brooklyn. Most include a list of streets, city charter, public services and a map. Advertising appears in 1822. No directory was published in 1827-1828, 1911, or between 1914 and 1932. The 1933 and 1934 directories listed here are New York City directories that include Brooklyn.

did.physloc: The microfilmed Brooklyn directories are stored at the Central Library outside the Center for Brooklyn History morgue.
The microfilmed New York City directories are stored at the Central Library outside the Center for Brooklyn History morgue.
The original bound volumes are stored at the Central Library, shelved outside the Center for Brooklyn History morgue.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Only available if the digital, microfilm, and facsimile versions cannot suffice.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The facsimile bound volumes are shelved inside the Center for Brooklyn History morgue at the Central Library.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Only available if the digital and microfilm versions cannot suffice.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These directories were published by George T. Lain, Lain & Healy, and George Upington and they list the names of Brooklyn's wealthiest householders classified by streets. They also include shopping guides and elite business directories. Flatbush and Long Island are listed separately in some volumes.

did.physloc: The microfilmed directories are stored at the Central Library.

did.unittitle: New York Telephone Directories, including Brooklyn White Pages

did.physloc: The microfilmed directories are stored at the Center for Brooklyn History.
The bound volumes are stored at the Central Library, shelved outside the Center for Brooklyn History morgue.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Only available if the microfilm and digital versions cannot suffice.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Brooklyn Classified Telephone Directories were published by the New York Telephone Company. They include alphabetized classified listings by subject. Most include advertising, maps of rapid transit lines, "Key to Street Numbers in Manhattan," U.S. Postal information, telephone equipment/services, calendar, and restaurant guide.

did.physloc: The microfilmed directories are stored at the Central Library.
The bound volumes are stored at the Central Library, shelved outside the Center for Brooklyn History morgue.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Only available if the microfilm cannot suffice.

did.physloc: The microfilmed directories are stored at the Center for Brooklyn History.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These directories were published by the New York Telephone Company and list Brooklyn and Queens subscribers together. Most include telephone information (instructions), toll rates, and lists of public pay stations.

did.physloc: The bound criss-cross directories are stored at the Central Library, shelved outside the Center for Brooklyn History morgue.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These directories were published by Cole's.
